item.title: Alan Cumming from the show "Tip Toe" - pronouns and gender identity have always been a conservative problem.
item.description: I adore him, and as someone who isn't generally sexually attracted to men, his version of the Caberet host on Broadway is why I went from saying "not sexually attracted to men" to "not USUALLY attracted to men." ? FP EDIT: shit. I just thought a few people might feel a little more seen and enjoy the clip. Happy pride month everyone! And always remember, Stonewall was a riot. Be brave, love, and love yourselves!
item.dc:creator: torisenblack

item.title: How?

item.title: They Voted No.
item.description: From page edit: Few of you are getting two states confused because there was another post earlier about a singular individual who voted no in Minnesota. Although this other state, Missouri, starts with the letter M, I assure you they're drastically different places. You know why. Michael Davis (R) Ben Whaley (R) Bryant Wolfin (R)

item.title: Just unrelated memes <.<
item.description:
The legal system is designed to protect the powerful from you, not the other way around
Sometimes that looks like bribing the government to keep their ability to extort you
Other times it’s removing your rights to make sure you don’t get any ideas
If only there was someway to fix all that, hmmm
Billionaires (and trillionaires now I guess) all got that way from the exploitation inherent to capitalism btw…
The green part is how they become billionaires. Yes we could tax them out of existence, we could also just stop them from taking what’s rightfully ours in the first place.
If you’re wondering what that looks like:
item.dc:creator: Mgxr44

item.title: Cooper the dog's family welcomed Winston the kitten in early 2025 and they became inseparable. One day on May 2026 Winston went missing
item.description:
Cooper kept waiting for Winston outside where they played together everyday https://www.instagram.com/reels/DYSjZ7SvVED/
Four days later Winston had returned home and Cooper was so happy https://www.instagram.com/reels/DYUnoiio4c3/

item.title: A Master Class in Rock Guitar from a Master
item.description: In one of the coolest moments in rock history, Eddie Van Halen stood inside the Smithsonian’s National Museum of American History and broke down exactly how he invented his legendary finger-tapping technique. He explained how he stumbled on the idea as a kid — inspired by piano and classical techniques — and later saw Jimmy Page playing with one hand and turned it into the explosive sound that changed guitar forever. Then he plugged in and unleashed “Eruption” live, right there in the museum. Watching Eddie demonstrate the birth of modern tapping in such a historic setting? Pure magic. That two-handed technique went from his bedroom to stadiums worldwide and influenced an entire generation of players. Eddie didn’t just play guitar — he rewrote the rules. One of the greatest innovators rock has ever seen. Rest in power, EVH.
item.dc:creator: LogicDude
